methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pivotal is a growth-stage transportation company founded by Marcus Leng and backed by Page. The valuation universe comprises five private peers in the transportation sector—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Peer-median valuation methodology is inapplicable to Pivotal given the sparse and heterogeneous peer set. The transportation sector yielded only five private companies total, with zero identified at the same stage, one at an adjacent stage, and zero within a similar valuation tier. Standard triangulation requires a minimum of three datapoints meeting narrow stage and scale filters; this dataset does not satisfy that threshold. A broad sector median would risk anchoring to fundamentally dissimilar business models and capital structures across the peer set.

Without sufficient comparable data, a defensible valuation range cannot be established through market-based methods. Further work should focus on identifying additional growth-stage transportation companies with disclosed valuations or financials, or pivoting to cost-of-capital and precedent-transaction methodologies if transaction history exists. The key uncertainty remains the lack of granular financial and valuation data from the identified peer set. Until that information is accessible, quantitative analysis of Pivotal's fair value cannot proceed with confidence.
